intro: You've felt it before — a scene comes on screen and a song hits and everything opens up. That's not an accident. That's music supervision.
quote: Music supervision is the art of finding the right song for the right moment — and the craft of making sure it can legally live there.

pillars.label: what it actually is
pillars.title: The Bridge Between Music and Storytelling
pillars.body: A music supervisor works at the intersection of creative vision and industry infrastructure. We source music for film, TV, advertising, and games — finding tracks that don't just *fit* a scene, but *deepen* it. It demands **an enormous library in your head, genuine emotional intelligence, and a working knowledge of music law** — all at once.

pillars.label: why it matters culturally
pillars.title: Music Supervision as an Act of Preservation
pillars.body: Music supervisors are **cultural gatekeepers**, whether we like it or not. Done thoughtfully, **it's one of the most powerful tools we have for keeping musical cultures alive and in conversation with each other.**

steps.title: Creative Brief
steps.body: Understanding the story — the tone, the era, the character's emotional state, what the director wants the audience to feel.

steps.title: Search & Discovery
steps.body: Digging through catalogs, reaching out to publishers and indie artists, pulling from years of deep listening. This is where taste lives.

steps.title: Clearance & Licensing
steps.body: Every song has two copyrights — master and composition. Both need clearing. Negotiating with labels, publishers, and sometimes the artists themselves.

steps.title: Cue Sheets & Administration
steps.body: After the show airs, cue sheets are filed with PROs so that everyone who made the music actually gets paid.
